snippets: The Stadtbrille is a bridge located in Amberg.
The Stadtbrille spans the Vils river.
The Stadtbrille creates a reflection in the water.
The reflection of the Stadtbrille forms the shape of spectacles.
The Stadtbrille is considered the symbol of Amberg.
The Stadtbrille is one of the most photographed sights in the city.
The Marktplatz is located in the old town of Amberg.
The Marktplatz is the central square of the old town.
The Marktplatz is surrounded by historic buildings.
The Rathaus is located on the Marktplatz.
The Rathaus is a Gothic building.
The Rathaus is a landmark of the old town.
The Rathaus is a visual centerpiece of the Marktplatz.
The Stadtmauer is the medieval city wall of Amberg.
The city wall of Amberg is one of the best-preserved in Bavaria.
Visitors can walk along sections of the city wall.
The city wall includes multiple towers.
The city wall forms part of the historic fortifications of Amberg.
The Bergtor is a historic city gate in Amberg.
The Bergtor is part of the former city fortifications.
The Vilstor is a historic city gate in Amberg.
The Vilstor is part of the former city fortifications.

Mariahilfberg is a pilgrimage destination.
Mariahilfberg is a popular destination for visitors.
Mariahilfberg offers panoramic views over Amberg.
The Mariahilfkirche is located on Mariahilfberg.

The Mariahilfkirche is a prominent landmark on the hill.

St. George’s Church is a Gothic parish church.
St. George’s Church is one of the major ecclesiastical landmarks in Amberg.
The Kurfürstliches Landrichteramt is a historic administrative building.
The building dates from the period of the Bavarian Electorate.
The Zeughaus is a historic building in the old city.
The Zeughaus is also known as the Arsenal.
The Erzbergkeller are underground vaults in Amberg.
The Erzbergkeller are connected to the city’s iron ore mining history.
